text: vv. 3, 4: Some divide these two sentences differently, and interpret thus: ‘It was through him that all things came into being, and without him came nothing. What was in him was life, and that life was the light of men’.
‘Master it’ may be taken in the sense of overcoming it, or of understanding it.
‘Their birth came’; several of the early Fathers seem to have used a text which read ‘His birth came’.
Some of the best manuscripts here read ‘God, the only-begotten’ instead of ‘the only-begotten Son’.
That is, the prophet whose coming was foretold by Moses in Deut. 18.15-19.
‘First of all’; some have thought that the other disciple was St John, who then calledhis brother St James. But the contrast may be with verse 43 below.
This is variously explained as referring to the Ascension, or to the Last Judgement.
